purpose: Parse YAML pipeline manifests into normalized step graphs, validate dependencies, and surface cycles or malformed references before orchestration.

endpoints.description: Parse and validate a YAML pipeline manifest into a normalized graph model.

dependencies.purpose: Parse YAML pipeline manifest documents into typed JavaScript objects

sidecar_specification.purpose: YAML pipeline manifest parser and dependency validator.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a workflow definition is stored as YAML and needs normalization before orchestration or catalog indexing.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for arbitrary YAML storage, runtime execution itself, or binary pipeline package parsing.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Provide a YAML manifest. 2) Call /pipeline-manifests/parse. 3) Use the normalized manifest and topological order in orchestration logic.

input_example.manifest_yaml:
id: demo
steps:
  - id: a
    image: alpine
  - id: b
    image: alpine
    depends_on: [a]

failure_modes.condition: Manifest omits required fields, references a missing step, or contains cycles
failure_modes.symptom: 400 error with the specific validation failure
failure_modes.recovery: Correct the manifest before passing it to the orchestrator

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use js-yaml to parse, require at minimum an id and a steps array, normalize missing step ids or names deterministically, and validate dependency references plus cycles with Kahn's algorithm.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Pipeline authors often mix image and container_image fields, omit step ids, or create hidden cycles through depends_on. Normalize first, then validate.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return a normalized manifest shape that later modules can consume directly without reparsing raw YAML.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not execute the pipeline or pull container images in this module.

body.manifest_yaml:
id: demo
steps:
  - id: a
    image: alpine
    depends_on: [b]
  - id: b
    image: alpine
    depends_on: [a]
